title: Tap Activity & Wear
composes: LossAnalysis2Tab (LossInspectorCard + TapPositionGauge + LossDriverRail + LossTimelineCard + TransformerLossPerformanceMap, recharts ComposedChart/Bar/Line/Scatter, AiSummary)
evidence: LossAnalysisTab.tsx:770 default export LossAnalysis2Tab — a [380px | 1fr] grid. Left: LossInspectorCard (:244) with a Loss-Inspector headline + Total/Efficiency tiles, a copper/core/stray/aux composition bar, a 2×2 mode picker (total/copper/core/stray), a Tap Position Optimization block mounting the catalog TapPositionGauge (:361 currentTap), a focus block (Load/K-Factor/RUL), an AiSummary (:388) driver line, and a LossDriverRail (:403). Right column splits into LossTimelineCard (recharts ComposedChart :701 — stacked copper/core/stray/aux Bars + efficiency/load Lines on dual axes, click-to-select period, legend rail) and TransformerLossPerformanceMap (recharts Scatter performance map). Periods are built by buildLossPeriods (:118) seeded off useAssetContext().assetId; preset/range/resolution selectors drive the timeline labels.
card_purpose: Counts the transformer's OLTC tap-changer operations through the day (hourly bars plus a cumulative lifetime counter), derives peak-hour and average activity, and converts the running total into a contact-life-remaining wear verdict against the tap contact's rated operations.
user_question: How often is this transformer's tap changer operating, when does the activity peak, and how much tap contact life remains?
inputs: Hourly tap_changer_operations_count series for the selected transformer (24 today-buckets plus the cumulative lifetime counter); derived peak-hour and average-operations KPIs; the tap_contact_life_max rated-operations constant; user-selected time mode and Hourly/Daily sampling (display-only picker).
output_insight: A ChartBars timeline of hourly tap operations with a cumulative right-axis line, Total / Peak-hour / Average KPI tiles, a FillBar contact-life-remaining verdict ('/5 million left'), and an AI wear narrative covering the peak hour, today's total and the wear percentage.
decision_support: Flags abnormal tap hunting (excessive operation counts or peak-hour clustering) for voltage-regulation investigation, and tracks contact wear so maintenance can schedule OLTC inspection or refurbishment before the contact life runs out.
relationship_to_page: The Tap/RTCC tab of the transformer detail view — the tap-changer activity and wear lens, beside Tap Position Optimization, the Voltage Regulation Timeline and Recent Tap Changes.
sem_purpose: Track tap-changer operation counts and derive contact-wear / contact-life-remaining verdicts
sem_answers: How many tap operations occurred, when activity peaked, and how much tap contact life remains
sem_supports: loss reduction, tap optimization, efficiency improvement, harmonic/K-factor mitigation, de-rating
sem_related: copper loss, core loss, stray loss, auxiliary loss, efficiency, voltage regulation, K-factor, HLF, RUL, tap position
visualization: Hourly bar chart (24 today-buckets) with a cumulative-total line on a dual axis, crosshair tooltip and interactive today/total legend rail; KPI row (total / peak hour / average); contact-life FillBar verdict; AI summary line.

title: Thermal Life
composes: ThermalLifeTab (Thermal Stress rail + KPI cells + AiSummary + recharts ComposedChart + ThermometerGauge x4 + ThermalPeakHeatmap)
evidence: ThermalLifeTab.tsx:40 default export — live thermal data on a 2-second setInterval (winding/oil/hotspot/ambient, activePower, currentLoad) with IEEE C57.91 efficiency/aging math. Row 1 "Thermal Life & Derating": a left rail with a Thermal-Stress headline + segmented stress bar + 3 KPI cells (Efficiency / Life remaining / Derated capacity) + AiSummary (:375), beside a recharts ComposedChart (:383 — derated-load Bar + hotspot/oil/efficiency Lines on dual temp/pct axes, hotspot-warn + efficiency-floor + selected-slot reference lines, click-to-select slot, legend rail). Row 2: four ThermometerGauge gauges (:481 Winding/Oil/Hotspot/Ambient, 0–100°C, status colour + peak) and ThermalPeakHeatmap (:538 rolling 24-hour component × time-slot grid). Catalog imports from components/charts: ThermometerGauge, ThermalPeakHeatmap, AiSummary, generateThermalData, normalizedHeatmapRow.
card_purpose: Tracks the transformer's instantaneous thermal state and life consumption — hotspot/oil/winding temperatures, thermal-stress %, IEEE C57.91 aging and remaining life, derated capacity and efficiency — over a live 2-second feed.
user_question: Is the transformer running thermally safe, how fast is it aging, and how much derated capacity is left?
inputs: Live winding/oil/hotspot/ambient temperatures (°C), active power and current load, with derived efficiency (IEEE C57.91), aging-rate multiplier, used/remaining life, thermal-stress %, derated capacity and headroom; a rolling per-component heatmap history.
output_insight: A thermal-stress rail with a segmented loss/load/ambient/headroom bar, Efficiency / Life-remaining / Derated-capacity KPI cells, an AI stress summary, a hotspot/oil/load/efficiency timeline against warn and floor reference lines, four live thermometer gauges, and a rolling peak-temperature heatmap.
decision_support: Tells operators whether to derate load or escalate cooling before the hotspot crosses warn/critical, and frames life-aging for maintenance and replacement planning.
relationship_to_page: The Thermal & Life tab of the transformer detail view — the thermal/aging lens, complementary to the Loss Analysis and Energy & Power tabs.
sem_purpose: Live thermal-stress, temperature, aging and derated-capacity health
sem_answers: Is the transformer thermally safe and how fast is it aging?
sem_supports: Transformer thermal loading, derating, and life/maintenance planning
sem_related: hotspot temperature, winding temperature, oil temperature, thermal stress, kVA headroom, aging factor, remaining life, efficiency
visualization: Two-row thermal composite — a Thermal-Stress rail with a segmented contributor bar, three KPI cells and an AI summary beside a recharts dual-axis ComposedChart (derated-load bars + hotspot/oil/efficiency lines, warn/floor/selected reference lines, legend rail); below, four ThermometerGauge gauges (0–100°C, status colour, peak marker) and a rolling ThermalPeakHeatmap component × time-slot grid.
